pages.full_text: [Printed brochure page glued in — no handwritten diary text. Printed headings and body text from a museum guide: FRENCH DOLLS; ART DECO GLASS (mentions René Lalique, Ernest Sabino); NANCY SCHOOL GLASS (mentions Emile Gallé, Daum brothers, Majorelle, Legra, Paul Nicolas); PERFUME BOTTLES (mentions René Lalique, Coty, Houbigant, Worth, Guerlain, Gallé, Daum brothers, Baccarat, Saint Louis). Photos of a bisque-headed doll, Art Deco glass vase, amber lamp, and green perfume bottle.]
pages.notes: Entire left page is a printed museum brochure/guide page glued into the diary. No handwritten diary text on this page. Printed content relates to Museo Art Nouveau y Art Deco, Casa Lis, Salamanca.

pages.full_text:
We drove to the end of the Roman Bridge at Salamanca again + walked across + up into the Museum of Art Nouveau + Art Deco in the Casa de Lis. The perfume bottles are lovely the dolls. We spent quite a time discussing them.
We had lunch on the pavement + drove to a le Clerk super market.
place_mentions: Museum of Art Nouveau + Art Deco
pages.notes: Page is mostly a printed museum brochure (PORCELAIN section and CHRISELEPHANTINES section) glued in, with handwritten diary text written over the middle portion. Handwritten page number '5' visible at top right. No year is stated on this page; the visit is to Salamanca, Spain — consistent with volume B (2003–2019) but the year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. 'le Clerk' is likely 'Leclerc' (French supermarket chain). The sentence 'The perfume bottles are lovely the dolls.' appears to be a compressed note, possibly 'The perfume bottles are lovely [as are] the dolls.' — transcribed verbatim.
